findings.name: Apache Log4j Remote Code Execution (Log4Shell)
findings.description: Apache Log4j2 2.0-beta9 through 2.15.0 JNDI lookup feature used in configuration, log messages, and parameters does not protect against attacker controlled LDAP and other JNDI related endpoints. An attacker who can control log messages or log message parameters can execute arbitrary code loaded from LDAP servers.
findings.solution: Upgrade Apache Log4j2 to version 2.17.1 or later. Set log4j2.formatMsgNoLookups=true as a JVM argument as a temporary mitigation.

findings.name: ProxyShell: Microsoft Exchange Server RCE
findings.description: Microsoft Exchange Server remote code execution vulnerability allowing pre-authentication attackers to execute arbitrary code via a crafted HTTP request to the Autodiscover endpoint.
findings.solution: Apply Microsoft Security Update KB5001779 or later. Ensure Exchange is updated to a patched version.

findings.name: OpenSSL Heap Buffer Overflow (CVE-2022-0778)
findings.description: The BN_mod_sqrt() function in OpenSSL could crash when given a non-prime modulus. Attackers can trigger infinite loop using specially crafted certificates, causing denial of service.
findings.solution: Upgrade OpenSSL to version 1.0.2zd, 1.1.1n, or 3.0.2 or later.

findings.name: Sudo Privilege Escalation (Baron Samedit)
findings.description: Sudo before 1.9.5p2 has a heap-based buffer overflow, allowing privilege escalation to root via sudoedit -s and a command-line argument that ends with a single backslash character.
findings.solution: Update sudo to version 1.9.5p2 or later. Run: apt-get update && apt-get upgrade sudo

findings.name: Samba Remote Code Execution (SambaCry)
findings.description: Samba since version 3.5.0 is vulnerable to remote code execution vulnerability, allowing a malicious client to upload a shared library to a writable share, and then cause the server to load and execute it.
findings.solution: Update Samba to version 4.6.4, 4.5.10 or 4.4.14. Add 'nt pipe support = no' to smb.conf as temporary mitigation.

findings.name: SSH Weak Cipher Algorithms Enabled
findings.description: The SSH service on this host supports weak cipher algorithms including arcfour, 3des-cbc, and blowfish-cbc which may allow a remote attacker to decrypt traffic.
findings.solution: Disable weak ciphers in /etc/ssh/sshd_config. Add: Ciphers aes128-ctr,aes192-ctr,aes256-ctr,aes128-gcm@openssh.com,aes256-gcm@openssh.com

findings.name: MySQL Unpatched Authentication Bypass
findings.description: Vulnerability in the MySQL Server product allows a low privileged attacker with network access via multiple protocols to compromise MySQL Server and read sensitive information.
findings.solution: Update MySQL Server to version 8.0.28 or later.

findings.name: Nginx Denial of Service via Crafted Request
findings.description: NGINX Open Source before versions 1.23.2 and 1.22.1, NGINX Open Source Subscription before versions R2 P1 and R1 P1, and NGINX Plus before versions R27 P1 and R26 P1 have a vulnerability in the module ngx_http_mp4_module that might allow a local attacker to corrupt NGINX worker memory, resulting in its crash or potential other impact using a specially crafted audio or video file.
findings.solution: Upgrade nginx to version 1.23.2, 1.22.1, or later.

findings.name: PHP Remote Code Execution via Deserialization
findings.description: In PHP versions before 7.4.30, 8.0.20, and 8.1.7 when PDO ODBC is used, specially crafted connection strings may cause out-of-bounds reads in addition to a possibly remote code execution.
findings.solution: Upgrade PHP to version 7.4.30, 8.0.20, 8.1.7 or later.

findings.name: Apache httpd mod_lua Use-After-Free
findings.description: A carefully crafted request body can cause a read to a random memory area which could cause the process to crash.
findings.solution: Update Apache httpd to version 2.4.53 or later.

findings.name: OpenSSH Information Disclosure
findings.description: The PKCS#11 feature in ssh-agent in OpenSSH before 9.3p2 has an insufficiently trustworthy search path, leading to remote code execution if an agent is forwarded to an attacker-controlled system.
findings.solution: Upgrade OpenSSH to version 9.3p2 or later.

findings.name: SSL/TLS BEAST Attack Vulnerability
findings.description: SSL 3.0 and TLS 1.0 are vulnerable to BEAST attack. The CBC cipher suite is affected by this attack.
findings.solution: Disable SSL 3.0 and TLS 1.0. Enable TLS 1.2 and TLS 1.3 only.

findings.name: Linux Kernel Privilege Escalation (Dirty Pipe)
findings.description: A flaw was found in the way the flags member of the new pipe buffer structure was lacking proper initialization in copy_page_to_iter_pipe and push_pipe functions in the Linux kernel. An unprivileged local user could use this flaw to write to pages in the page cache backed by read only files and use it for privilege escalation.
findings.solution: Update Linux kernel to 5.16.11, 5.15.25, or 5.10.102 or later.

findings.name: Redis Unauthenticated Access
findings.description: Redis is accessible without authentication. An attacker could read sensitive data or use Lua sandbox escape to execute arbitrary code.
findings.solution: Configure Redis to require authentication. Set requirepass in redis.conf. Bind Redis to localhost only.

findings.name: HTTP Strict Transport Security (HSTS) Missing
findings.description: The remote web server does not send the HTTP Strict-Transport-Security (HSTS) header. HSTS protects against SSL stripping attacks and ensures browsers only connect over HTTPS.
findings.solution: Add the HSTS header: Strict-Transport-Security: max-age=31536000; includeSubDomains

findings.name: FTP Anonymous Login Permitted
findings.description: The FTP server allows anonymous access. An unauthenticated attacker can read files accessible to the FTP anonymous account.
findings.solution: Disable anonymous FTP access. If FTP must be used, require authentication for all access.

findings.name: SMTP Open Relay
findings.description: The SMTP server appears to allow relaying from external hosts. This could allow attackers to send spam email through this server.
findings.solution: Configure SMTP relay restrictions to allow only authenticated or local relay.

findings.name: SSH Server CBC Mode Ciphers Enabled
findings.description: SSH server is configured to use CBC mode ciphers. This may allow an attacker to recover plaintext from the ciphertext.
findings.solution: Edit /etc/ssh/sshd_config to disable CBC ciphers.

findings.name: HTTP Server Default Credentials
findings.description: The web server login page was accessible with default credentials admin/admin.
findings.solution: Change all default credentials immediately. Enforce strong password policy.

findings.name: ICMP Timestamp Request Remote Date Disclosure
findings.description: The remote host answers to an ICMP timestamp request which can allow an attacker to determine the system's clock.
findings.solution: Filter ICMP timestamp requests using a firewall rule.
